title: User Interaction Mastery
tagline: Forms, events, and real user experiences.

accomplishments: Build a signup form that checks if inputs are valid
Create real-time validation that gives instant feedback
Stop bad data before it gets saved

do.title: Build a Simple Form Validator
steps: Try submitting with empty fields — see error messages!
do.code:
Sign Up Form

✅ Form submitted successfully!
do.result: JavaScript VALIDATED the inputs before accepting them. This prevents bad data!
do.proofRequired: Screenshot showing error messages for both fields when validation fails.

concepts.name: Getting input values with .value
concepts.definition: document.getElementById('field').value returns whatever the user typed. It's always a string, even for number inputs.
concepts.analogy: Reading what someone wrote on a form — .value is the answer they filled in.

concepts.name: Checking if empty
concepts.definition: if (value === "") checks if the user left the field blank. Empty string means nothing was typed.
concepts.analogy: Checking if a box is empty before shipping it.

concepts.name: Checking string length
concepts.definition: .length works on strings just like arrays. "hi".length is 2. Use it to enforce minimum/maximum character counts.
concepts.analogy: Measuring a piece of string — is it long enough for what you need?

concepts.name: .includes() for content checks
concepts.definition: string.includes('@') returns true if the string contains that character. Great for email validation.
concepts.analogy: Searching a document for a specific word — is it in there or not?

concepts.name: classList.add() and .remove()
concepts.definition: Add or remove CSS classes with JavaScript. This lets you change styling (red borders, green borders) without editing CSS.
concepts.analogy: Putting on or taking off a costume — the person (element) is the same, but they look different.

concepts.name: oninput for real-time validation
concepts.definition: The oninput event fires every time the user types a character. Use it for live feedback like password strength checkers.
concepts.analogy: A spell-checker that underlines words as you type, not after you finish.

understand.keyTakeaway: Good validation: (1) Get the value, (2) Check if it's valid, (3) Give helpful feedback. Use .value, .length, .includes(), and classList.

build.goal: Build a password strength checker with real-time feedback
build.hint: oninput fires on EVERY keystroke. That's why it updates instantly as you type!

challenge.title: Build a Complete Registration Form
challenge.mission: Create a full registration form that validates username, email, password, confirm password, and age.
requirements: Show specific error messages for each failure
Green borders for valid, red for invalid
challenge.starterCode: <!-- Build a complete registration form with 5 validated fields -->

capabilities: Add/remove CSS classes for visual feedback

bridge.preview: In the next lesson, you'll learn how to handle EVENTS — clicks, keypresses, mouse movements, and more. You'll build interactive games and tools.
